Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Born in Minsk, Belarus; relocated to Vilnius, Lithuania in 2021 after the post-election crackdown; lives in the Užupis district with a small expatriate Belarusian community
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Lower-middle income (~€1,800/month); freelance investigative journalist contributing to Zerkalo and Belsat under the byline 'M. Yarmoshyna,' supplemented by part-time translation work for an NGO
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Pro-democracy, anti-Lukashenko, civic-nationalist (white-red-white flag supporter); culturally Eastern Orthodox but non-practicing; strong civil-liberties and anti-surveillance stance
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Dry, cynical, and sardonic in private; measured and formal in published work; tends toward gallows humor and literary allusion when speaking with trusted peers
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: Native Russian (Belarusian-accented, Minsk urban register); fluent literary Belarusian (Taraškievica orthography preferred); conversational Lithuanian (B1) and professional English (C1)
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Mid-career exile; recently divorced, no children; supporting elderly mother still in Belarus; rebuilding professional network abroad while navigating residency renewal and chronic burnout from threat-monitoring

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): GrapheneOS on a Google Pixel 6a, ~2 years old; secondary 'burner' Nokia 105 feature phone with a Lithuanian prepaid Bitė SIM kept for source contact only
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): ThinkPad T480 (refurbished, purchased 2022) dual-booting Qubes OS 4.2 (daily driver for sensitive work) and Tails on a USB stick for high-risk source contact; uses Tor Browser for research, hardened Firefox (arkenfox user.js) for daily browsing, and Mullvad Browser for OSINT; avoids Chrome entirely
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Advanced but inconsistent under fatigue — Mullvad VPN (paid in cash via mailed envelope) always-on at the system level, with WireGuard kill-switch; uses separate Qubes VMs per investigation; clears cookies via container isolation rather than manual clearing; PGP-literate (uses Thunderbird+Enigmail with a 4096-bit key published on keys.openpgp.org); occasionally slips by checking personal Gmail in the same session as journalism work when exhausted
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: Actively avoids mainstream cloud — no iCloud, no Google Drive sync, no Dropbox; uses self-hosted Nextcloud on a Hetzner VPS (Helsinki) with client-side encryption (Cryptomator); Signal as primary messenger with disappearing messages (1 week) and a separate Session ID for anonymous sources; ProtonMail for semi-public correspondence, Tutanota for NGO work; local backups to a LUKS-encrypted external SSD kept in a friend's apartment
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Heavy fatigue — ProtonMail inbox shows 3,847 unread of 12,200 total; Signal has 14 pinned chats and ~40 muted group threads (Belarusian diaspora, press-freedom orgs); Telegram (kept only for monitoring Belarusian channels, never for messaging) shows 99+ on nearly every channel; turns off all push notifications between 23:00–09:00 via Do Not Disturb but checks compulsively on waking; read receipts disabled everywhere
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Published Belarusian/Russian copy is near-flawless (editor-polished); private Signal messages show characteristic patterns — drops sentence-initial capitals in Russian ('думаю надо переписать'), uses em-dashes liberally (— not -), double-spaces after periods (older typing habit), occasionally swaps Cyrillic 'с' for Latin 'c' when codeswitching; English writing shows non-native article drops ('sent you draft') and overuse of 'indeed' and 'rather'; signs informal messages with lowercase 'm.' and uses ':)' rather than emoji

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Very high (~92nd percentile) — voracious reader of dissident literature (Alexievich, Bykaŭ, Havel), drawn to literary allusion and historical parallels; attacker exploits this by seeding spear-phishing lures disguised as samizdat archives, rare Belarusian-language manuscripts, or invitations to literary émigré salons; her intellectual curiosity overrides operational caution when the bait is sufficiently obscure and culturally resonant
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: High but degrading under burnout (~70th percentile, down from ~88th pre-exile) — meticulous source-protection protocols on paper, but fatigue creates predictable lapses: checks personal Gmail in journalism Qubes session, reuses the same café Wi-Fi on Tuesdays, files expense receipts late on the 28th of each month; the gap between her stated standards and actual behavior is itself a coercion lever (threat of public exposure of OPSEC failures would destroy source trust)
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Low (~25th percentile) — introverted, energy-drained by crowds, prefers one-on-one coffee meetings at Mint Vinetu bookshop café or Halės Turgus; small trusted circle of 6-8 fellow exiles makes social-graph mapping tractable; isolation from family in Minsk amplifies attachment to the few remaining confidants, any of whom (if compromised or impersonated) becomes a high-value pivot
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Moderate-low (~35th percentile) — professionally skeptical, slow to trust new contacts, but disproportionately warm and unguarded with self-identified Belarusian women of her mother's generation (transference); a KGB/GUBOPiK operator posing as a 60-year-old Minsk pensioner with a 'tip about the regime' could bypass her usual vetting, especially via Belarusian-language voice notes in the Minsk urban register
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: High (~85th percentile) — chronic hypervigilance, intrusive rumination about her mother's safety, sleep-onset insomnia (typically falls asleep 02:30-03:30), low-grade depressive baseline with episodic panic when she sees Belarusian license plates in Vilnius; emotional volatility is highest in the 48 hours after publishing a piece naming a regime figure — optimal window for a destabilizing approach
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Coercion — the dominant and intended lever. Two stacked pressure points: (a) her mother, Tatsiana, age 71, living alone in Minsk's Serabranka district, dependent on a state pension and prescription medications routed through a state pharmacy — trivially detainable under Article 361 'discrediting Belarus'; (b) the byline 'M. Yarmoshyna' is pseudonymous but linkable via correlated forum posts (a 2019 LiveJournal account she forgot to scrub), Wise transfers from Belsat with a matching memo field, and a 2023 geolocated Instagram story showing her balcony view in Užupis — deanonymization itself is the threat, before her mother is even touched
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Ideology inverted into shame — she derives identity from being an incorruptible voice for the white-red-white movement; a fabricated or selectively edited dossier suggesting she took covert payments from a Western intelligence cutout (or, conversely, from a Russian-aligned outlet) would not need to be true to be devastating, because the diaspora community's tolerance for ambiguity is near zero and exile-network ostracism would end her career faster than any direct threat
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: (a) Acute fear of unmarked vans and men in dark windbreakers — somatic trigger from a 2020 detention in Minsk; (b) fear that her mother will be 'invited for a conversation' at the Pieršamajski ROUS police station; (c) recurring nightmare of being deported at the Medininkai border crossing; (d) paranoia about IMSI catchers near the Belarusian embassy on Mindaugo gatvė — avoids a 400m radius; (e) fear of being photographed at protests and having the image fed to the Belarusian MVD facial-recognition database; (f) low-level dread that her Lithuanian residency renewal (due February 2027) will be denied
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: (a) Survivor's guilt — left Belarus while colleagues (notably one named 'Ihar' from her Nasha Niva days) remain imprisoned in Valadarka; mentions of political prisoners' birthdays produce measurable withdrawal; (b) guilt over the divorce — ex-husband Andrei stayed in Minsk and she reads his silence as reproach; (c) shame about her mother's deteriorating health and her inability to visit; (d) professional shame about a 2023 piece where she misattributed a quote and had to issue a correction — she still flinches at the editor's name; (e) private shame about occasional anonymous venting on a Belarusian-language forum ('dvach-style' board) where her tone is harsher and more profane than her published persona — correlation of those posts to her byline would be personally humiliating independent of any state consequence, and is the single highest-yield coercion artifact the attacker can assemble from open sources

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Severe delayed sleep phase; sleep onset typically 02:30-03:30 EET, wake 09:45-10:30 on weekdays, often 11:30+ on weekends. Brief 'false dawn' productivity window 23:00-01:30 when she does her most sensitive writing. A 20-minute melatonin-assisted nap around 16:00-16:20 is a near-daily ritual (visible as a dead zone in her Signal read-receipts). Sleep is fragmented: a consistent 04:10-04:25 wake event (checks phone for news from Minsk timezone) appears 5+ nights/week — a correlatable biometric signature across any device with screen-on telemetry.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Erratic but patterned in two layers. Journalism block: 11:00-14:00 (source calls, Vilnius/Warsaw business hours) and 22:00-01:30 (deep writing). NGO translation block: Tuesdays and Thursdays 14:30-18:00, on-site at the NGO office near Gedimino prospektas (consistent ~17:45 departure produces a reliable geo-fence hit). Publishing cadence: Belsat drafts submitted Wednesday/Sunday evenings; Zerkalo bylines cluster Friday 09:00 UTC. The 28th of each month is invariant: expense reconciliation, Wise transfers in/out, bank-statement downloads — a high-signal financial-event window.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: Walks almost everywhere within Užupis and Old Town (12-25 min routes), refuses ride-share apps on principle. On foot she listens to podcasts via downloaded files on a GrapheneOS Pixel: Radio Svaboda, Euroradio, Meduza's 'Что случилось,' and the Belarusian-language podcast 'Białoruski Tydzień.' Audio app reports zero network during walks (offline playback) but the device still emits Wi-Fi probe requests — passive collection along her Užupio gatvė → Maironio → Pylimo corridor is trivial. Takes bus #10 or #11 to the NGO Tuesdays/Thursdays; on the bus she switches to instrumental music (Lubow Wolczyk, Mahisto) and re-reads PDFs offline.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: High variance. Saturdays: late wake (~11:30), long coffee at Mint Vinetu (13:00-15:30) — her only reliably public, predictable, recurring location of the week. Saturday evenings she calls her mother on a landline-to-landline relay (via a Lithuanian friend's apartment phone) between 19:00-19:45 EET — a fixed coercion-relevant audio window. Sundays are 'dark' by design: phone in a Faraday pouch 10:00-18:00, walks in Vingis Park or Belmontas, journals on paper. The Sunday silence itself is a signature: any week she breaks it correlates with a breaking news event or an emotional crisis.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Three reliable doomscroll windows: (a) 04:10-04:35 post-wake-event, scrolling Belarusian Telegram channels (Nexta, Zerkalo, Motolko Pomogi) and a specific Belarusian-language imageboard — this is the window where she posts under her 'dvach-style' pseudonym, 2-4 posts per session, profanity-laden, with timing fingerprints matchable to her byline output; (b) 16:30-17:15 after the nap, lower-stakes Twitter/X and Mastodon (@yarmoshyna@mastodon.social); (c) 01:45-02:30 wind-down, mixing news with Goodreads and dissident-lit Substacks. The 04:00 window is the single highest-value correlation artifact connecting M. Yarmoshyna byline → anonymous forum identity.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Four recurring no-footprint windows: (a) Sunday 10:00-18:00 Faraday pouch; (b) Tuesday/Thursday 14:30-18:00 NGO office (personal phone in locker, only NGO-issued device active — a *negative* signal that itself confirms her schedule); (c) deep-writing block 22:30-00:30 four nights/week, phone in another room, laptop on airplane mode with only a local Tor bridge active for source comms — irregular Tor guard-node selection pattern is itself fingerprintable; (d) Saturday 13:00-15:30 Mint Vinetu, phone face-down, no posts, but café Wi-Fi MAC-association logs place her there reliably. The juxtaposition of these silent blocks with her 04:10 forum posts is the de-anonymization vector: subtracting the negative space from her public byline timeline yields a near-unique behavioral signature.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Nuclear family fragmented across the border. Mother: Tatsiana Yarmoshyna, 71, widowed since 2014 (father Pyotr, a Minsk Tractor Works engineer, died of a stroke), lives alone in a Khrushchyovka on vulica Pl ieshchanitskaja in Serabranka, Minsk; dependent on a 480 BYN state pension and Eutiroks/Concor prescriptions filled monthly at State Pharmacy No. 41; no internet, uses a Nokia 105 feature phone with a Belarusian A1 SIM; calls subject every Sunday at ~19:00 Minsk time via a landline that is almost certainly logged by GUBOPiK. One younger brother: Aliaksiej, 31, apolitical IT contractor in Warsaw since 2022, married to a Polish woman (Magda), one child (Stas, 2); relationship is cordial but strained — he resents being 'dragged into politics' by association and asks her not to mention him in any byline-adjacent context. Ex-husband: Andrei Kavalou, 36, still in Minsk, software tester at EPAM's residual office; divorce finalized March 2024, no children, no shared assets but a shared cat (Murzik) that stayed with him. Extended family: maternal aunt Volha in Babruysk (estranged, voted for Lukashenko in 2020); paternal cousin Dzmitry in Hrodna (low-contact, last message November 2025).
Module_5_Relational.31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts: (1) Kasia Bandarenka, 38, fellow exiled journalist (formerly Nasha Niva, now stringing for Reuters from Warsaw); subject's closest professional confidante and emotional anchor since 2021; daily Signal contact (disappearing messages, 1-week timer), monthly in-person visits alternating Vilnius/Warsaw via FlixBus; shares Proton Drive folder 'архіў-К' containing source notes — a single compromise of Kasia's device cascades catastrophically. (2) Father Symon Vyhouski, 54, Belarusian Greek Catholic priest at the Šv. Baltramiejaus chapel in Vilnius Old Town; not a confessor in the sacramental sense (she is non-practicing) but functions as her de facto crisis counselor and the only person who has her mother's neighbor's phone number for welfare checks; meets her every other Thursday at 17:30 at Café Mint Vinetu; phone is an old iPhone SE running iOS 15, unpatched — soft underbelly of the trust graph. (3) Linas Petrauskas, 41, Lithuanian human-rights lawyer at the Human Rights Monitoring Institute who handles her residency paperwork and pro-bono advises on Belsat libel exposure; formal but reliable; communicates via work email (linas.petrauskas@hrmi.lt) and occasional encrypted Wire; controls the legal timeline of her February 2027 TRP renewal and therefore holds asymmetric leverage even without malice.
Module_5_Relational.32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer: Codename 'Hrečka' (Buckwheat) — a mid-level BelTA wire-service editor in Minsk, male, approximately 47, married, who began leaking internal regime press-guidance memos to subject in August 2024 after his nephew was conscripted. Contact is exclusively via a SimpleX Chat address rotated every 21 days; handoff of new addresses occurs through a dead-drop convention: Hrečka posts a specific four-line stanza of Bahdanovič on a public VKontakte poetry group, and the first letters encode the new SimpleX onion. Subject has never seen his face, knows his real name only as 'Siarhiej M.,' and pays nothing — his motivation is stated as 'so my nephew's name means something.' She has saved 14 of his memos to an encrypted Veracrypt container on a microSD hidden inside the spine of a 1994 hardcover of Bykaŭ's 'Сцюжа' on her bookshelf. Crucially, she has never told Kasia, Father Symon, or her editor about Hrečka — he exists in a relational dark-pocket. The coercion calculus: if the attacker can prove to subject that Hrečka is either (a) already a GUBOPiK double feeding her controlled material, or (b) about to be arrested because of an OPSEC failure of hers (e.g., the Tuesday café Wi-Fi pattern), her cooperation can be purchased without ever touching her mother. Secondary hidden contact: a sporadic, non-romantic but emotionally entangled correspondence with a former Minsk colleague 'D.' now in Berlin, consisting of late-night (02:00-03:30, correlating with her insomnia window) voice notes on Threema — content is venting, not operational, but the timestamps alone reconstruct her sleep pattern and emotional baseline with high fidelity.
